key_insights.insight: Entrepreneurship doesn't have to start with a grand vision; it can emerge gradually from monetizing existing skills or content.
key_insights.quote: It doesn't always need to be this one big decision... it can just be, you know, something you're just doing in that moment.

key_insights.insight: The polished image of successful businesses often hides the initial, often imperfect, steps and products.
key_insights.quote: I look back at some branding, some content, some all of that and I look at it and I'm like it was crap.

key_insights.insight: There needs to be a middle ground between hustle culture and self-care culture by finding what helps one maintain their balance.
key_insights.quote: Sometimes self-care is the most productive thing you can do, but also sometimes productivity is an act of self-care.

key_insights.insight: Boundaries between work and personal life are eroding, leading to burnout and decreased productivity, especially in remote work environments.
key_insights.quote: Why am I working more than I ever have and producing less than I ever have and also my mental health is on the floor?

key_insights.insight: As a CEO, it is important to hire people better than you in their respective fields. By relinquishing total control, a leader empowers their team and unlocks the company's full potential.
key_insights.quote: You should hire people that are better than you at their specific disciplines. So like, even if you're like, if you're micromanaging, if you're being like a bottleneck, you are automatically causing a problem.

key_insights.insight: You cannot have the business win the glory or you win the glory, it is a combination of both in order to succeed.
key_insights.quote: Would you like the business to have the glory or would you like you to have the glory and like then realize that if the business has the glory you still have the glory but you just have to wait a bit.

key_insights.insight: To improve self-validation, one must be understanding of themselves, understand they are going to do things wrong, and be completely in touch with themselves.
key_insights.quote: It's an understanding of yourself it's an understanding that you're going to fuck up it's an understanding that you're going to think one thing and do another.

actionable_advice.advice: Implement mental health days within your company.
actionable_advice.how_to: Designate specific days where employees can take time off specifically for mental wellbeing, and make it clear that this is supported by the company culture.
Enroll employees automatically in therapy programs. Giving them the option to opt out instead of signing up creates a safer space for people to feel okay to participate.
Delegate tasks at work to team members and then fully step back. Provide support when asked but offer to have the team member make the call instead.

memorable_stories: Grace's physical body manifested seizures when she was ignoring the warning signs of her mental health.
Grace describes nearly deciding to work instead of watching a movie with a housemate because she wanted to get ahead on tasks.
contrarian_claims: Toxic positivity is okay if that's how you cope and the only thing that matters is to be yourself even if it is controversial.
The idea that high profile people have to show the best of their lives but it's okay to post very differently on social media.
It's okay to have two or three businesses that are doing well even at the same time and not pursue a new business just to do it.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Grace Beverley: How To Build A Multi-Million Pound Empire At 24
